name: [NeuroQuest API] device-signature pass rate SLO — signed-login success slowly drifting down

search.query: service:neuroquest-api env:production ("device_signature:missing" OR "device_signature:invalid" OR "device_signature:expired" OR "device_signature:id_mismatch" OR "device_signature:issued_at_too_old" OR "device_signature:replayed")

options.escalation_message: Device-signature pass rate is STILL below the SLO. Use the dashboard's route × hard_mode breakdown to isolate the degrading surface; set DEVICE_AUTH_SOFT_MODE=1 for the general gate, or revert the offending build for the strict GDPR/IAP gates (they ignore soft mode).

_runbook.why: The device-signature-lockout monitor only fires on a SUDDEN spike of active-install rejections in a 15m window. A gradual erosion of the pass rate (99.8% drifting toward 97% over days, or one route quietly degrading) never trips it — it would only be visible to someone watching the device-signature pass-rate dashboard. This ratio-based SLO monitor closes that gap so slow regressions page automatically.
_runbook.math: Reuses the SAME sample-scaled pass rate as the dashboard: scaled_ok = ok / sample_rate; pass_rate = scaled_ok / (scaled_ok + rejected). Datadog formula: 100 * (ok / rate) / ((ok / rate) + rejected). Successes are SAMPLED (device_signature:ok, ~1%) so the raw ok count understates true success volume; scaling by 1/sample_rate recovers it before forming the ratio.
_runbook.sample_rate_source: `rate` = avg(@extra.sample_rate) over the device_signature:ok captures — the LIVE DEVICE_AUTH_SUCCESS_SAMPLE_RATE emitted on every ok capture, NOT a hardcoded 0.01. This keeps the scale-up correct after the env var is changed, matching the dashboard exactly. @extra.sample_rate must exist as a Datadog measure facet for the avg() to evaluate.
_runbook.rejected_signal: Rejections counted: missing, invalid, expired, id_mismatch, issued_at_too_old, replayed — identical to the dashboard. no_server_key is EXCLUDED (that verdict is allowed through as misconfig, not a login failure). Unlike the spike lockout monitor, this SLO does NOT filter on @extra.active_install: the pass rate is a fleet-wide ratio and the pre-handshake 'missing' tail is already diluted by the (far larger) scaled success volume in the denominator.
_runbook.window_and_thresholds: 4h evaluation window matches the dashboard's 'Overall pass rate (last 4h)' tile and is long enough to average out 1%-sampling noise in the ok count. warning=99 / critical=98 sit well below the ~99.8% baseline but well above the 97% drift the task calls out, so a slow slide pages with headroom. notify_no_data=false (low-traffic lulls produce 0/0, not a real regression). If sampling noise causes flapping at low traffic, widen the window (e.g. last 12h/24h) rather than lowering the threshold.
_runbook.rollback: General per-:id gate: set DEVICE_AUTH_SOFT_MODE=1 (no redeploy) to return requireDeviceSignature to verify-and-log-without-reject. Strict GDPR (requireDeviceSignatureStrict) and IAP (requireUserOrDevice) gates IGNORE soft mode by design — revert the offending build instead. See .agents/memory/device-signature-enforcement.md.
_runbook.provisioning: Auto-applied to the live Datadog account by scripts/provision-monitors.ts on the api-server production build, keyed by the injected monitor_key:device-signature-pass-rate-slo tag. Do NOT hand-create in the Datadog UI. See .agents/memory/datadog-monitor-provisioning.md.
